author: Snifer
description: Streamline solo and group TTRPG journaling with Lonelog notation shortcuts and templates. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Snifer (Bastion del Dinosaurio)
manifest.description: Streamline solo and group TTRPG journaling with Lonelog and Partylog notation shortcuts, dashboards, and templates.
manifest.name: Lonelog

name: Lonelog

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Feature: Partylog Add-on for Group Session Notes

Added the first Partylog release inside the existing Lonelog plugin as an activable add-on for group-session notation, parsing, and dashboard-driven review.

### What Changed

- Added a new **Partylog add-on** toggle in plugin settings.
- Registered fenced `partylog` code blocks alongside `lonelog` blocks.
- Added syntax highlighting support for Partylog world events using `!`.
- Introduced a dedicated `PartylogParser` for extracting timeline, roster, and scene-aware dashboard data from Partylog notes.
- Added a new **Partylog Dashboard** with internal tabs for:
  - `Overview`
  - `Scenes`
  - `Threads`
  - `Timeline`
  - `Roster`
  - `Recap`
- Added Partylog-focused test coverage for tokenizer and parser behavior.
- Documented Partylog activation and usage in both English and Spanish READMEs.
- Updated plugin metadata and badges to version `1.6.0`.
- Fixed `version-bump.mjs` so new releases are added to `versions.json` by version key instead of incorrectly checking only `minAppVersion`.

### Release Notes

- This is a **minor release** because it adds a substantial new user-facing feature set rather than a small patch fix.
- Build and test artifacts should be regenerated in a fully enabled local environment before publishing the release bundle.

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Inventory Parser Fixes: Bundles, Slot Updates, and Property Mutations

Fixed multiple inventory parsing issues so shorthand item updates, bundle notation, slot-based inventory, and item property mutations now behave consistently with the add-on manual.

### What Changed

- Normalized bundle notation in item names so forms like `[Inv:Arrow×20]` and `[Inv:Arrowx20]` register item `Arrow` with quantity `20`.
- Fixed shorthand inventory deltas so `[Inv:Torch+2]`, `[Inv:Torch +2]`, and `[Inv:Torch|+2]` follow the same update flow.
- Routed logical item updates such as `[Inv:Torch-1]`, `[Inv:Torch+2]`, `[Inv:Torch|0]`, and `[Inv:Torch|depleted]` through existing slot/container entries instead of creating duplicate root items.
- Added deterministic multi-slot consumption and update behavior when the same logical item exists in more than one slot.
- Fixed inventory property mutation semantics so `+prop`, `-prop`, `prop->new`, and `prop→new` now work for `[Inv:]` items just like entity tags.
- Ensured property mutations still apply on inventory lines that also include absolute quantities such as `[Inv:Short Sword|1|+enchanted]`.
- Aligned release metadata and documentation badges to version `1.5.7`.
- Closes #36.
- Closes #37.

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Resource Tracking Fixes: Decimal Wealth and Slot-Based Inventory

Fixed two resource-tracking issues in the parser: decimal wealth deltas were truncating fractional values, and slot/container-based inventory entries could not be consumed through shorthand updates like `[Inv:Torch-1]`.

### What Changed

- Fixed `[Wealth:...]` delta parsing so decimal balances are preserved when applying signed updates such as `[Wealth:Bank -1]`.
- Reworked decimal arithmetic to stay compatible with the current ES6 TypeScript target without relying on `BigInt`.
- Extended `[Inv:...]` parsing so shorthand consumption can resolve item quantities nested inside slot/container entries such as `[Inv:Backpack 1|Torch×6]`.
- Added multi-slot consumption support using a deterministic first-mentioned-first-consumed strategy when the same item exists in more than one slot.
- Updated nested slot quantities to re-render correctly after consumption, including converting exhausted slots to `empty`.
- Aligned release metadata and documentation badges to version `1.5.6`.
- Closes #34.
- Closes #35.

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Support Obsidian version 1.12.7

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Entity Merge Fix: Lonelog Threads PC State

Fixed entity tag merging so `Lonelog Threads` no longer drops previously known PC or NPC attributes when a later mention only updates one field.

### What Changed

- Updated the parser merge logic for repeated entity tags such as `[PC:...]`, `[N:...]`, and `[L:...]`.
- Changed partial entity updates to merge by attribute key instead of replacing the full tag list.
- Preserved existing values like `HIT`, `GRIT`, and `WILL` when a later tag only updates one field.
- Kept support for additive (`+`), removal (`-`), and replacement (`->`) tag update semantics.
- Added parser tests covering partial PC updates and attribute replacement behavior.
- `Lonelog Threads` now shows stable combined entity state across multiple mentions.
- Closes #32.
- Closes #33.

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Compatibility Update: Obsidian API and Popout Windows

Repeated the compatibility adjustments from `1.5.2` as part of the latest release cycle, reflecting the changes that were made to metadata and UI behavior.

### What Changed

- Kept `minAppVersion` aligned with the current compatibility baseline used by the plugin.
- Preserved the Obsidian API compatibility adjustments around modern workspace and frontmatter helpers.
- Kept the popout window compatibility fixes based on `activeDocument`, element-owned documents, and window-scoped timers.
- Retained the settings tab rendering cleanup that avoids internal direct calls to `display()`.
- Registered this release as a follow-up compatibility update in the changelog.
- Documented the compatibility changes again under `1.5.3`.
- Kept the release history aligned with the latest metadata and implementation changes.

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Compatibility Update: Obsidian API and Popout Windows

Updated the plugin metadata and UI code to match the Obsidian APIs already used by Lonelog, and improved compatibility with popout windows.

### What Changed

- Raised `minAppVersion` to `1.2.1` to match the current use of modern Obsidian APIs such as `processFrontMatter`, `workspace.activeEditor`, and newer workspace helpers.
- Replaced direct `document` access with `activeDocument` or element-owned documents where needed, so overlays, settings rendering, and syntax highlighting behave correctly in popout windows.
- Replaced global `setTimeout()` calls with window-scoped timers for popout window safety.
- Removed internal direct calls to `display()` inside the settings tab implementation while keeping the `display()` override for backward compatibility with older Obsidian versions.

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Performance Fix: IME and CJK Input Lag

Improved editor responsiveness when typing with IME-based keyboards such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean input methods.

### What Changed

- Skipped expensive editor highlighting rebuilds while CodeMirror is in text composition mode.
- Prevented autocomplete from reparsing the full document during IME composition.
- Optimized autocomplete so a full Lonelog parse happens once per autocomplete session instead of on every query update while typing inside the same tag context.

### Result

- Reduced typing lag with IME composition.
- Lowered unnecessary parsing work in large notes.
- Improved responsiveness when editing Lonelog tags such as `[N:...]`, `[PC:...]`, `[Thread:...]`, and similar entities.

---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Syntax Highlighting Fix: Multi-line Entity Tags

Fixed syntax highlighting for multi-line bracket entities such as `PC`, `N`, and other Lonelog tags, so the tag scope is now preserved across lines in both the editor and reading view. (Reported by @loukaka in #30.)

**Before:**

Multi-line forms like:

```lonelog
[PC:Jonah
| trait: friendly, curious
| status: wounded
| stat: HP 8, Stress 2
]
```

were parsed correctly by the Lonelog engine, but the highlighter failed to keep the tag scope active across lines in the editor and reading mode.

**Now:**

- Highlighting is preserved from the opening `[` line through the closing `]`.
- Tokenization now supports multi-line tag context consistently in both editor highlighting and reading mode.

### Settings UI Redesign

Redesigned the Lonelog settings tab from a single long scrolling page into a **sidebar + content panel** layout for faster navigation as the plugin grows.

### What Changed

- Reorganized settings into 7 navigable sections:
  - `Interface`
  - `Notation`
  - `Templates`
  - `Colors`
  - `Dice`
  - `Add-ons`
  - `About`
- Moved syntax editor controls into `Interface`.
- Moved all highlighting color controls into a dedicated `Colors` panel.
- Added visible `Active` / `Inactive` badges for add-ons.
- Moved `About` to the end of the navigation.
- Added a version badge in the `About` panel using the plugin manifest version.


---

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Feature: Fractional Track Progress

Added support for fractional (decimal) values in track progress, enabling systems like **Ironsworn** that advance progress in partial steps instead of whole integers.

**Examples:**

```
[Track:Find who killed my uncle 1.5/10]
[Track:Bonds 0.25/4]
[Track:Vow 0.5/10]
```

Inline update syntax also supports fractional values:

```
[Track:Find who killed my uncle 0.5/10 ->1.5/10]
```

> Source: [Reddit feedback from an Ironsworn user](https://www.reddit.com/r/lonelog/comments/1shof87/comment/onx1ohc/)

---

### CI/CD & Repository Infrastructure

- **GitHub Actions Release Workflow Update (`release.yml`)**:
  - Configured the workflow to extract specific release notes from `CHANGELOG.md` to populate the GitHub Release body automatically.
  - Added build artifact attestation (`actions/attest-build-provenance`) for secure verification of generated files.
  - Ensured `CHANGELOG.md` presence validation before finishing builds.

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
- Fixed Advanced Dice Notation (kh, kl, dh, dl) calculation where the total sum was incorrect.
- Resolved an issue in the formatting engine that caused advanced results to be replaced by basic dice sums.
- Enhanced dice result visualization: now all rolled values are displayed (e.g., "6, 2, 1"), but only the kept values are calculated in the final total.
- Improved parsing stability when using modifiers (e.g., +1, -2) alongside advanced dice rules.
releases.name: New Release (Fix Advance Dice Notation)

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## [1.4.5] - 2026-04-21
### ✨ New Features

* **Advanced Dice Notation Addon Support**: Lonelog now natively supports complex dice syntax (inspired by rpg-dice-roller). Enable it in settings to use:
  * Drop/Keep mechanics: (e.g., `4d6dl1` to drop the lowest, or `2d20kh1` for advantage).
  * Exploding Dice: (e.g., `2d6!`).
  * Success Counting & Targets: Complex comparators like `3d20>=12`.
* **Reading Mode Card Support**: The interactive Card draw widget 🎴 (draw/tarot) now properly renders when viewing logs in Reading Mode inside code blocks.

### Bug Fixes

* Fixed inconsistencies between Obsidian's Live Preview and native Reading Mode. Interactive widgets are now reliably injected without breaking the DOM.
releases.name: Advanced Dice Notation Addon Support (1.4.5-dev)

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Snifer/lonelog/compare/1.4.3-dev...1.4.4-dev
releases.name: Fix problem in Dungeon addon 1.4.4-dev

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## 🃏 What's New / Changelog (Card Notation Add-on)

### ✨ New Feature: Full Card Add-on Support

We have officially integrated dedicated functionality for drawing and managing playing cards, perfectly adhering to the "Card Notation Add-on Specification."

- **New Dedicated Engine:** Designed a standalone random generation engine separate from the dice roller. It natively supports both the standard French deck (`54 cards`, including Red/Black Jokers) and the full Tarot deck (`78 cards`, Major and Minor Arcana, with a 30% chance for automatically reversed cards).

### Live Interactivity (Widgets & Commands)

- **Dedicated Card Widget:** By typing `d: draw`, `d: tarot`, or defining assignments like `d: Past=tarot`, the Live Preview editor will dynamically inject a floating card widget (🎴). Clicking on this widget will perform the draw live on the line.
- **Multiple Draw (Spreads) Support:** The system fully understands complex spreads and multiple variable assignments on the same line (e.g., `d: Past=tarot, Present=tarot, Future=tarot`) and correctly generates distinct results for all targets simultaneously.
- **New Palette Command:** Added the _“Draw card on current line”_ command. This allows users to bind card drawing actions directly to keyboard shortcuts, bypassing the mouse completely.

### Enhanced Readability & Auto-Translation

- **Automatic Inline Comments:** No more memorizing what `M16r` or `8Cu` stand for. When drawing a card, the plugin will now automatically append the semantic name of the card as a standardized inline comment. Example: `d: draw=Qs // Queen of Spades` or `d: tarot=M16r // The Tower reversed`.
- **Description Toggle (Optional Settings):** For purists who prefer raw and minimal outputs without comments (`d: draw=Qs`), there's a new toggle in the settings called `Inline card descriptions` to easily turn off the automated translations.

### Visual Polishing & Modularity

- **Smart Visual Cleanup:** Resolved an issue where a redundant "classic dice button" would persist on lines with finished/resolved card draws. Now, once a card is successfully drawn, the widget gracefully vanishes instantly to keep the log flawlessly clean.
- **Full Opt-in Control (Modular Add-on):** Staying true to Lonelog's philosophy, the entire Card system is a modular Add-on. To unlock these features, users navigate to the Lonelog plugin settings and toggle on `Enable Card Notation Add-on`. If left disabled, it adds zero bloat to the base flow.
releases.name: Support Card Add-on Support v1.4.3-dev

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## 🚀 What's New / Changelog (v1.4.2)

### Visual & Typography Customization

- **New Font Configuration Options:** You can now fully customize the reading experience explicitly for your `lonelog` code blocks through the plugin settings:
    - **Font Family Override:** Easily bypass Obsidian's default monospace font with your preferred typeface (e.g., _Consolas_, _Fira Code_).
    - **Font Size:** Directly adjust the text scale specifically for your Lonelog narrative blocks (`px`, `em`, `%`).
    - **Token Boldness Toggle:** Added a "Token Font Weight" selector to tone down the default boldness of syntax highlights. You can now switch between "Bold" (default) and "Normal" for a softer, more uniform look.
- **Strict CSS Encapsulation Fix:** Resolved an issue where font sizing and family configurations would bleed into the rest of Obsidian's Reading Mode when the _Global Notation_ toggle was ON. All Lonelog typographical customizations are now strictly isolated to actual Lonelog blocks.

### Resources & Inventory Track Add-on

- The heavily anticipated **Inventory and Wealth** toolset is fully operational! Bridging the gap between narrative flow and character sheets.
- **Syntax Highlighting & Commands:** Added visual tracking and quick-insert editor commands for `[Inv: item]`, `[Wealth: gold]` tags, as well as the summary state block `[RESOURCES]`.
- **Interactive Tracker View:** Introducing the new _Resources & Inventory Tracker_ dedicated tab. This dynamically updating sidebar panel automatically extracts and organizes all aggregated supplies, key items, and currencies registered throughout your campaign log.

### Autocomplete Engine Overhaul

- **Complete Autocomplete Logic Rewrite:** The pop-up suggestion system has been rebuilt to be faster and significantly more robust.
- **Missing Tags Restored:** Added robust suggestion support for **Inventory** (`[Inv:...]`), **Wealth** (`[Wealth:...]`), and **Foes** (`[F:...]`).
    - _Foes_ are now dynamically aggregated from past combat encounters (`[COMBAT]`), pulling their custom stats natively into the suggestions box—perfect for referencing recurring enemies!
- **Global Reference Support Fixed:** Previously, the autocomplete would fail to trigger if you were making a reference using the `#` symbol for elements like Locations, Threads, PCs, or Clocks. Now, tags like `[#L:`, `[#Thread:`, and `[#PC:` will properly prompt suggestions.
- **Case-Insensitive Suggestions:** The autocomplete trigger is now fully case-insensitive. Typing `[inv:` or `[INV:` will work exactly the same without silently failing.
releases.name: What's New / Changelog (v1.4.2-dev)

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## [1.4.1] - 2026-04-16
### Added
- **Dungeon Crawling Add-on**: A new optional module for room and exploration tracking.
- **Dungeon Status View**: Dedicated sidebar panel to visualize all discovered rooms, their statuses, descriptions, and exits.
- **New Notation**: 
    - Room tags support: `[R:ID|status|description|exits]`.
    - Incremental status updates: `[R:ID|+status]`.
    - Dungeon status blocks: `[DUNGEON STATUS]...[/DUNGEON STATUS]`.
- **Dynamic Styling**: Added settings to customize highlighting colors for Room tags and Dungeon blocks.
- **Intelligent Autocomplete**: Suggests existing Room IDs and shows their current state while typing `[R:`.
- **New Commands**: 
    - "Insert room tag"
    - "Insert dungeon status block"
    - "Open dungeon status view"
### Changed
- Integrated Dungeon sections into the main **Dashboard View**.
- Updated Version to 1.4.1 in manifest and package files.
releases.name: Added Support Dungeon Crawling Add-on  [1.4.1-dev]

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
# **Fully compatible with Lonelog 1.4.0 system.**

#### ⚔️ Added

- **Combat Add-on (v1.0)**: New tactical combat management system!
    - **Notation Support**: Added support for round markers (`Rd#`) and combatant tags (`[F:]` for foes and `[PC:]` for players).
    - **Combat View**: A dedicated tracker to manage initiative order, HP, and Stress during encounters.
- **Lonelog Dashboard**: A premium, unified control panel to manage sessions, scenes, threads, and characters in a single view.
- **Ribbon View Selector**: A new sidebar icon and menu that allows you to quickly select and open any Lonelog view.
- **New Commands**:
    - `Initialize note properties`: Automatically injects the required YAML frontmatter for Lonelog notes.
    - `Open view selector`: Quick access to the view menu via the command palette.

#### 🛠️ Changed

- **UI Standardization**: Transitioned all interface labels and setting descriptions to **sentence case**, following official Obsidian design guidelines for a native look and feel.
- **Enhanced Syntax Highlighting**: Improved the precision and performance of the Live Preview and Reading Mode highlighters.

#### 🧹 Fixed

- **Comprehensive Code Audit**
- **Promise Handling**
- **Regex Consistency**

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Snifer/lonelog/compare/1.4.0-dev...1.4.0

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
This version introduces Lonelog's first Modular Add-on system, designed to handle tactical encounters without sacrificing the simplicity of the core notation.

### 🚀 Added

- **Combat Add-on**: A modular tactical encounter system (enable it via **Settings -> Add-ons**).
- **New Combat Notation**:
    - **Combat Blocks**: `[COMBAT]` and `[/COMBAT]` delimiters.
    - **Round Markers**: `Rd#` (e.g., `Rd1`).
    - **Foe Tags**: `[F:Name|Stats]` (e.g., `[F:Orc|HP 10]`).
    - **PC Tags**: `[PC:Name|Stats]`.
- **Combat Tracker View**: A new sidebar view that provides:
    - Current round display and combat status.
    - A dynamic roster of PCs and Foes with live stat tracking.
    - **Quick Navigation**: Click on any combatant's name to jump directly to their last entry in the log.
- **Quick Insertion Commands**:
    - `Insert Combat Block`
    - `Insert Round Marker`
    - `Insert Foe Tag`
- **Visual Customization**: Individual color settings for all new combat notation tags.

### 🔧 Fixed

- **Full Localization**: Resolved missing translation keys in both English and Spanish (Reading mode labels, Combat Tracker titles, etc.).
- **Dice Stability**: Fixed a bug in the "Roll dice on line" command to ensure reliable result insertion.
- **Hardened Parser**: Improved extraction logic for combatants and complex states inside tactical blocks.
releases.name: Release Log - v1.4.0 (Addons System)

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
Full support Lonelog 1.4

#### 🚀 New Features

- **Unified Lonelog Dashboard**: Introduced a new central tab view that provides a comprehensive overview of your entire campaign.
    - **Timeline Widget**: Interactive vertical view of all sessions and scenes.
    - **Live Progress**: Visual tracking for clocks, tracks, and timers with progress bars.
    - **World & Cast**: Unified gallery for NPCs, Locations, and Plot Threads.
- **Intelligent Scene Auto-increment**:
    - **Thread Support**: Automatically detects and increments parallel threads (e.g., `T1-S1` → `T1-S2`).
    - **Flashback Support**: Handles flashback sequences with letter suffixes (e.g., `S5a` → `S5b`).
- **New Command: "Return from Flashback"**: A specialized command that terminates a flashback sequence and automatically calculates the next main sequence scene (e.g., `S5c` → `S6`).

#### 🔧 Fixes & Improvements

- **Shared Data Architecture**: Refactored the internal parser to centralize session and scene detection, ensuring 100% data consistency between sidebars and the new Dashboard.
- **Dashboard Auto-Refresh**: Implemented a debounced auto-update system that keeps the Dashboard in sync as you edit your notes without impacting performance.
- **TypeScript Hardening**: Resolved several null-safety and strict-typing issues across the codebase to ensure more robust execution.
- **Refined I18n**: Completed English and Spanish localizations for all new dashboard widgets and scene commands.
releases.name: Lonelog v1.3.4 - Release Notes

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## Lonelog v1.3.3 - Release Notes

#### 🚀 New Features

- **Reference Tags Support**: Added support for reference tags using the `#` prefix (e.g., `[#N:NPC Name]`). These correctly register as entity mentions within the plugin's database.
- **Tag-Based Sidebar Navigation**: Introduced a new **"Tags" section** in the Thread Browser side panel. It aggregates all entity traits into an interactive tag cloud, allowing you to filter and navigate your world by tags.
- **Intelligent Generator Blocks (`gen:`)**:
    - **Automated Table Resolution**: Generator sub-lines (e.g.,  `Appearance: d4`) now automatically look up and resolve results from matching tables (`tbl: Appearance`) defined in your notes.
    - **Block Rolling**: Clicking the dice icon on a `gen:` header now triggers a multi-roll for all indented child lines at once.
    - **Reading Mode Support**: Rollable generator blocks and individual dice buttons are now fully functional in **Reading Mode (Preview)**.

#### 🔧 Fixes & Improvements

- **Unified Roll Engine**: Implemented a shared `RollManager` to ensure consistent dice rolling and table resolution across the command palette, Live Preview, and Reading Mode.
- **Resolved View Duplication**: Fixed a race condition that occasionally caused side panels (Threads, Progress, Scenes) to render content twice.
- **Improved Prefix Detection**: Anchored dice prefixes (`d:`, `tbl:`, etc.) to the start of the line to prevent corruption of labels containing the letter 'd' (e.g., `Attitude:`).
- **Notation Preservation**: Fixed a bug where dice notation (e.g., `d20`) was removed when inserting the roll result. It now formats as `d20=15`.
- **Better I18n**: Added localized strings for the new Tags navigation in both English and Spanish.
releases.name: Lonelog v1.3.3 dev

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## Fixes & Improvements

- Closes #13 — Fix dice widget click in edit mode (Live Preview) + Re-add `lonelog: dice roller` command to palette
- Closes #14 — Fix autocomplete not triggering for `Clock:`, `[E:`, ` [Track:` and `[Timer: prefixes
releases.name: 1.3.2-dev

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Fixed syntax highlighting for `## Session XX` headers and narrative lines starting with `S`
- Fixed an overly broad regex pattern that was incorrectly matching any line beginning with the letter `S`, causing plain narrative text (e.g., `Ships at sea.`) to receive the blue highlight color reserved exclusively for `### Scene` headings.
- `## Session XX` and `### Scene XX` headers now render with their correct and distinct highlight styles.
- Closes #11

### Enabled text wrap for narrative blocks in lonelog code blocks
- Fixed missing text wrap inside `lonelog` blocks. Long narrative lines were overflowing horizontally, forcing the user to scroll sideways to read content in both Live Preview and Reading Mode.
- Applied `white-space: pre-wrap` / `word-wrap: break-word` so that narrative text wraps naturally, consistent with regular paragraph text in Obsidian.
- Closes #12

## Fixes & Improvements

- Closes #4 — Allow inline progress updates for Clocks and Tracks from later log entries
- Closes #6 — Support named random tables and direct die resolution
- Closes #7 — Fix dice notation formatting for inline results
- Closes #8 — Show all dice results inline instead of only the last roll
- Closes #9 — Display individual dice values for multi-die rolls
releases.name: 1.3.1-Dev

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
## Changes

- Fixed multiple reported issues:
  - #4
  - #6
  - #7
  - #8
  - #9

## Details

This release focuses on bug fixes and stability improvements, with no breaking changes expected.
releases.name: 1.3.0-dev

releases.author: Snifer
releases.description:
# Lonelog for Obsidian v1.2.0 - Complete Solo TTRPG Suite

##  Funcionalidades
Este es el lanzamiento inicial completo del sistema Lonelog para Obsidian, integrando el estándar de notación para diario en solitario directamente en tu bóveda con extensiones potentes.

- **Sistema de Notación**: Atajos inteligentes para la simbología base @, ?, d:, ->, and =>. 
- **Mejoras Visuales**: Resaltado de sintaxis en tiempo real.
- **Vistas Laterales**:
  - **Progress Tracker**: Gestión de relojes y tracks.
  - **Thread Browser**:  Seguimiento de NPCs e hilos de historia.
  - **Scene Navigator**: Navegación entre sesiones.

- **Extensiones**:
  - **Combat**:  Seguimiento táctico y marcadores de ronda.
  - **Dungeon Crawling**:  Gestión de estados de mazmorra.
  - **Resource Tracking**: estión de inventario y riqueza.


## Instalación

### BRAT (recommended for beta testing)
- Instala el plug-in BRAT
- En configuraciones de BRAT settings, click en Add Beta Plugin
- Ingresa https://github.com/Snifer/lonelog
- Habilita Lonelog en Configuraciones > Plugins Comunitarios


### Instalación Manual 
- Este plugin se encuentra en desarrollo activo.
1. Descarga los ficheros `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` crea una carpeta de tu bóveda  .obsidian/plugins/lonelog/.
2. Actualiza los plugins en Obsidian
3. Habilita Lonelog en Ajustes → Plugins de la comunidad.


## English Release

This is the initial full release of the Lonelog system for Obsidian, bringing the standard solo journaling notation directly into your vault with powerful extensions.


###  Features 
- **Core Notation System**: Smart shortcuts for @, ?, d:, ->, and =>. 
- **Visual Enhancements**: Real-time syntax highlighting for Live Preview and Reading Mode. 
- **Side Views / Vistas Laterales**:
  - **Progress Tracker**: Manage Clocks and tracks visually. 
  - **Thread Browser**: Track NPCs and story threads.
  - **Scene Navigator**: Quick jump between game sessions. 
- **Advanced Add-ons (Integrated) / Extensiones**:
  - **Combat**: Tactical tracking and Round markers. 
  - **Dungeon Crawling**: Room state tracking. 
  - **Resource Tracking**: Inventory and Wealth management. 

## Install Plug-in

### BRAT (recommended for beta testing)
1. Install the BRAT plugin
2. In BRAT settings, click Add Beta Plugin
3. Enter https://github.com/Snifer/lonelog 
4.Enable lonelog in Settings > Community Plugins

### Manual Installation 
1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release.
2. Place them in your vault's path: `.obsidian/plugins/lonelog/`.
3. Enable "Lonelog" in **Settings → Community Plugins**.
